task_completion.description: Open the Epi Info 7 Classic Analysis module
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Epi Info 7 Classic Analysis window is visible on the screen.
The READ command is visible in the command log, or the symptom variables (Fever, Cough, SoreThroat, Dyspnea, Myalgia) appear in the variables list.

task_completion.description: Define a Group Variable named 'SymptomProfile' aggregating the five symptoms
task_completion.visual_evidence: The DEFINE GROUPVAR command is visible in the command log creating 'SymptomProfile' from the 5 symptoms, or the group variable creation dialog is shown with the correct variables selected.

task_completion.description: Perform a frequency analysis on the 'SymptomProfile' group variable
task_completion.visual_evidence: The FREQ command is executed on 'SymptomProfile' and a frequency table is displayed in the output window.

task_completion.description: Save the resulting report as an HTML file to the specified path
task_completion.visual_evidence: The ROUTEOUT command is used with the path 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\TaskData\symptom_report.htm', or a save dialog shows the file being saved to this exact location as an HTML file.

integrity.description: The agent used the Epi Info 7 Classic Analysis interface to perform the tasks
integrity.visual_evidence: Interactions occur within the Epi Info 7 GUI (menus, dialogs, or command prompt within the app), rather than writing an external script.

integrity.description: The frequency table was generated by the software's analysis engine
integrity.visual_evidence: The frequency table appears in the Epi Info 7 output pane as a result of a command, not manually typed or copy-pasted.

integrity.description: The agent did not manually create the HTML file using a text editor
integrity.visual_evidence: There are no text editors (like Notepad) open with HTML code manually written to fake the output.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Epi Info 7 is CDC epidemiology software. The Classic Analysis module supports the DEFINE GROUPVAR command to create group variables from multiple fields, the FREQ command for frequency analysis, and the ROUTEOUT command to save outputs as HTML. The dataset 'respiratory_cluster.csv' is synthetic. The VLM should verify that these specific commands or their GUI equivalents were used to generate the final HTML report.
